name: bandit-stealth
description: Free, local-first coding agent for VS Code — run Ollama models (Gemma, Qwen, Devstral) with tool use, diff approvals, and voice. Optional Bandit cloud.

banditStealth.model.description: Model name to request from the Bandit API or Ollama. `bandit-logic` is the recommended default — it's our agent-tuned wrapper around Qwen 3.6 27B, optimized for tool-use loops in the Bandit harness.

banditStealth.apiUrl.description: Bandit API URL. Trailing /completions or /chat/completions will be trimmed automatically.

banditStealth.embeddingsUrl.description: Base URL for the Bandit embeddings service used for repository indexing and semantic search.

banditStealth.intentUrl.description: Bandit intent endpoint used to interpret goals before completions.

banditStealth.pythonPath.description: Absolute path to a Python 3 executable. Leave empty to let Bandit Stealth auto-detect python3/python.

banditStealth.insightsAi.description: Whether the /insights command may ask the cloud LLM to summarize your aggregate session data. 'auto' = prompt once on first use and remember the answer. 'allow' = always include the AI summary. 'deny' = never include it. Local Ollama users skip the prompt entirely (data never leaves the machine).

banditStealth.feedbackUrl.description: Bandit feedback endpoint for chat ratings and diff reviews.

banditStealth.provider.description: LLM provider. ollama (default): local, private, free. bandit: hosted cloud inference. openai-compatible: any OpenAI-compatible endpoint — LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, OpenRouter, Together, Groq, DeepSeek, OpenAI proper. When openai-compatible is selected, also set banditStealth.openaiBaseUrl and banditStealth.openaiModel.

banditStealth.openaiBaseUrl.description: Base URL for an OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Examples: http://localhost:1234/v1 (LM Studio), http://localhost:8080/v1 (llama.cpp / vLLM), https://api.together.xyz/v1, https://openrouter.ai/api/v1, https://api.groq.com/openai/v1, https://api.deepseek.com/v1, https://api.openai.com/v1. The provider appends /chat/completions automatically. Required when provider is openai-compatible.

banditStealth.openaiApiKey.description: Bearer API key for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Local servers (LM Studio, llama.cpp) usually skip this. Stored in workspace settings — for stronger isolation, set OPENAI_API_KEY in your shell environment instead.

banditStealth.openaiModel.description: Model id sent to the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Each upstream uses its own naming — e.g. meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo on Together, openai/gpt-4o on OpenRouter, your local model name on LM Studio.

banditStealth.openaiHeaders.description: Extra HTTP headers sent to the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Useful for OpenRouter attribution headers (HTTP-Referer, X-Title), org IDs, etc. Authorization is set automatically from openaiApiKey.

banditStealth.ollamaUrl.description: Primary local Ollama endpoint (Mac localhost or LAN). Legacy alias for ollamaBaseUrl. Never point this at RunPod.

banditStealth.ollamaBaseUrl.description: Primary local Ollama endpoint (Mac localhost or LAN). Never point this at RunPod — use provider:bandit + model:bandit-core-2 for RunPod.

banditStealth.ollamaNodeUrl.description: Optional secondary Ollama endpoint for an external GPU node (e.g. RTX 5090). When set, Ollama requests are routed here instead of ollamaBaseUrl. Leave empty to use local only.

banditStealth.ollamaModel.description: Ollama model for local inference. Default: gemma3:12b (fits most machines). Set ollamaNodeUrl + this to gemma4:31b for GPU node inference.

enumDescriptions: Use the runtime default per model — off for reasoning-capable models (Qwen 3.x, DeepSeek R1) so agent tool-use stays fast. Recommended.
Force chain-of-thought thinking mode ON for every request. Slower first-token latency (8–30s) but may improve quality on hard refactor / planning tasks.
Force thinking OFF even for models where the runtime default would enable it.
banditStealth.thinkingMode.description: Chain-of-thought thinking for reasoning-capable models. 'auto' defers to per-model defaults; 'on' / 'off' are session-level overrides.

banditStealth.watchdogMs.description: No-token watchdog override in milliseconds. -1 uses Bandit's auto-sized window, 0 disables the watchdog, and any positive value pins the timeout. BANDIT_NO_TOKEN_WATCHDOG_MS still wins when set in the editor process environment.

banditStealth.voice.autoSpeak.description: Automatically read short assistant responses aloud using the Bandit cloud TTS endpoint. Only active when provider=bandit with an API key. Long responses (>400 words) and code-only replies are never auto-spoken — a manual speaker button on each message lets you play them on demand.

banditStealth.voice.maxAutoSpeakWords.description: Word-count cap for auto-speak. Responses longer than this are NOT auto-spoken (the speaker button still works for manual playback). Set to 0 to disable the cap.

banditStealth.voice.voiceId.description: ElevenLabs voice model used for TTS. Defaults to Brian. Hidden for now; will surface as a picker in a future settings pass.

banditStealth.voice.micEnabled.description: Enable the microphone button in the composer for speech-to-text transcription. Provider is configurable independently of the chat provider — see banditStealth.voice.stt.provider.

banditStealth.voice.stt.provider.description: Speech-to-text provider. 'bandit' uses the Bandit cloud STT endpoint (requires a Bandit API key). 'openai-whisper' targets any Whisper-compatible HTTP endpoint (faster-whisper-server, whisper.cpp HTTP, OpenAI, LiteLLM, vLLM). 'custom' lets you point at any URL with the same multipart shape.

banditStealth.voice.stt.url.description: STT endpoint URL. Required when provider is 'openai-whisper' or 'custom' (e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions, http://localhost:8000/v1/audio/transcriptions). Ignored when provider is 'bandit'.

banditStealth.voice.stt.apiKey.description: Bearer token for the STT endpoint when not using Bandit cloud. Stored in user settings (not VS Code Secrets) so it can travel with workspace settings — for production secrets prefer a self-hosted endpoint without a token.

banditStealth.voice.stt.model.description: Model name sent to OpenAI-compatible Whisper endpoints (e.g. 'whisper-1', 'whisper-large-v3'). Ignored by Bandit cloud and most self-hosted servers that take whatever model is loaded.

banditStealth.voice.tts.provider.description: Text-to-speech provider. 'bandit' uses the Bandit cloud (requires a Bandit API key). 'openai' targets /v1/audio/speech. 'elevenlabs' uses ElevenLabs' /v1/text-to-speech/{voice} URL. 'piper' is a local Piper HTTP server. 'custom' is a free-form URL + JSON body shape.

banditStealth.voice.tts.url.description: TTS endpoint URL or base URL. Required when provider isn't 'bandit'. For ElevenLabs leave empty to use the default https://api.elevenlabs.io; voiceId is appended at request time.

banditStealth.voice.tts.apiKey.description: Bearer token / API key for the TTS endpoint when not using Bandit cloud. Sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <key>' for OpenAI and 'xi-api-key: <key>' for ElevenLabs.

banditStealth.voice.tts.model.description: Model name for OpenAI-compatible TTS (e.g. 'tts-1', 'tts-1-hd') or for ElevenLabs ('eleven_monolingual_v1'). Ignored by Piper / Bandit cloud.

banditStealth.useTerminal.description: Prefer the terminal-based CLI experience. When on, the Bandit activity-bar icon opens the bandit CLI in VS Code's integrated terminal instead of the webview chat panel. Requires @burtson-labs/bandit-stealth-cli on PATH (npm i -g @burtson-labs/bandit-stealth-cli).

banditStealth.autoContextEnabled.description: Auto-attach relevant workspace files to each prompt. When on, Bandit scans your active editor, recently-opened files, and goal hints to pick up to 5 files likely to be useful. Off by default — auto-context can burn through thousands of tokens per turn before the model even sees your prompt. Prefer explicit @-mentions; turn this on when you want less typing and have headroom to spend.

banditStealth.ollamaHeaders.description: Extra HTTP headers sent on every Ollama request. Use for reverse-proxied / authenticated Ollama endpoints (Bearer token, Cloudflare Access, RunPod proxy, etc.). Content-Type is always application/json and cannot be overridden.

banditStealth.ollamaAutoRouteModels.description: Automatically route Ollama requests by input type: coding/text prompts use ollamaCodingModel, image prompts use ollamaVisionModel.

banditStealth.ollamaCodingModel.description: Preferred Ollama model for coding/text tasks. Leave empty to use ollamaModel — the previous default of qwen2.5-coder:14b silently overrode user selections when auto-routing was enabled, causing hangs for anyone without that model pulled.

banditStealth.ollamaVisionModel.description: Preferred Ollama model for image prompts. Leave empty to use ollamaModel. Set only when you want a different vision-capable model (e.g. llava) than your base model. Must support vision.

banditStealth.ocrFallback.description: How to handle images when the active model can't see them. ocr-first: try local OCR (Apple Vision on mac, tesseract elsewhere), inline the text, stay on the current model. vision-first: skip OCR, require a vision-capable model. off: drop images silently (legacy behavior).

banditStealth.agentOllamaModel.description: Optional explicit Ollama model override for agent runs. When empty, agent uses ollamaCodingModel when auto-routing is enabled.

banditStealth.enableToolUse.description: Enable text-based tool use in ask mode. When true, the model can call read_file, write_file, search_code, git_status, and other tools to resolve your request. Works with all Ollama models — no native function calling required.

banditStealth.embeddings.provider.description: Embeddings provider for semantic context search. 'ollama' uses local nomic-embed-text (already installed). 'bandit' uses the hosted Bandit embeddings API. 'none' disables semantic search and falls back to file-list context.

banditStealth.embeddings.ollamaModel.description: Ollama model for local embeddings. nomic-embed-text is already installed and recommended (274 MB, CPU-friendly).

banditStealth.context.preferGateway.description: When true and the current repo is indexed in GatewayApi, use Qdrant semantic search for context. Falls back to local nomic-embed-text when Gateway is unavailable or repo is not indexed.

banditStealth.gatewayUrl.description: GatewayApi base URL (e.g. https://api.burtson.ai). Required to use Qdrant semantic search and workspace binding. Leave empty to use local embeddings only.

banditStealth.workspaceId.description: GatewayApi workspace ID for the current repo. Auto-detected from git remote when possible. Set manually if auto-detection fails. Used for Qdrant context search and task/PR tracking.

banditStealth.temperature.description: Sampling temperature passed to the Bandit API.

banditStealth.topP.description: Top-p nucleus sampling value for the Bandit API.

banditStealth.systemPrompt.default: You are Bandit Stealth, an expert coding assistant that helps users write and reason about code inside VS Code.
banditStealth.systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt that is prepended to every request.

banditStealth.coauthor.description: When enabled, commits Bandit makes on your behalf include a `Co-authored-by: Bandit <bandit@burtson.ai>` trailer so GitHub shows the Bandit ninja avatar on PR / blame / contributor views. Set BANDIT_NO_COAUTHOR=1 in your editor's process environment to force off.

banditStealth.webSearch.tavilyApiKey.description: API key for the `web_search` tool (powered by Tavily — free tier at https://tavily.com). Without this, web_search will return a 'not configured' error so the agent falls back to web_fetch. Leave blank to disable. The TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable is checked as a fallback.

banditStealth.intent.showSuggestions.description: Show inline intent chips and autocomplete suggestions in the chat composer.

banditStealth.insertResponsesByDefault.description: When enabled, responses are inserted directly into the active editor instead of prompting.

banditStealth.agent.confidenceTarget.description: Confidence threshold to consider a goal achieved.

banditStealth.diff.reviewMode.description: Stream in-progress rewrites into the Diff Review panel while the agent drafts changes.

banditStealth.feedback.enabled.description: Show chat feedback prompts after assistant responses. Disable to hide the thumbs up/down row.

banditStealth.notifications.enabled.description: Show VS Code notifications when Bandit needs approval, fails, finishes a long/background task, or completes while the panel is hidden.

banditStealth.notifications.minTurnMs.description: Minimum agent-turn duration before Bandit shows a completion notification while the panel is visible. Hidden-panel completions always notify when notifications are enabled.

banditStealth.agent.dryRun.description: Preview agent writes without applying changes to disk so you can review diffs first.

banditStealth.debug.emitPlanJson.description: Save agent plan and report artifacts under .bandit/plans/. Disable to keep the workspace clean.

banditStealth.agent.createBranchBeforeRu.description: When enabled, the agent automatically creates a git branch (bandit/{goal-slug}) before starting a goal. Changes are isolated and easy to discard if the run goes wrong. Requires git to be installed and the workspace to be a git repository.

banditStealth.agent.autoApproveEdits.description: When enabled, write_file and apply_edit tool calls skip the in-chat permission card and land immediately. run_command always still prompts regardless of this setting.

banditStealth.agent.skipValidationInDev.description: When running the extension from source, skip TypeScript/package validation so local workspace errors do not block goals.

scripts.build:vsix: pnpm -C ../.. --filter @burtson-labs/agent-core build && pnpm -C ../.. --filter @burtson-labs/stealth-core-runtime build && pnpm -C ../.. --filter @burtson-labs/host-kit build && pnpm -C ../.. --filter @burtson-labs/agent-adapters-vscode build && pnpm run build:webview && pnpm run compile && pnpm run package:release
